docs.title: KonverterMap
docs.text:
KonverterMap is a lightweight, expressive and strongly-typed object mapping library for .NET.

✅ A simple, fast and flexible alternative to AutoMapper 🧠 Compatible with .NET 8 and .NET Framework 4.6.1+ (via .NET Standard 2.0)

docs.title: ✨ Features
docs.text:
• 📦 Minimal setup – just map and go
  • 🔒 Strongly typed property access (Expression<Func<>>)
  • 🧠 Custom logic with .ForMember(...)
  • 🙈 Property ignoring with .Ignore(...)
  • 🔁 Bi-directional mapping with .ReverseMap()
  • 🧩 Recursive mapping using Konverter.Map(...)
  • 📋 Conditional mapping with .When(...)
  • 🔄 Collections, nested objects and null safety
  • ⚙️ Available as a NuGet package

docs.title: 🚀 Why KonverterMap?
docs.text: Many developers rely on object mapping in layered architectures (DTOs, ViewModels, Entities). KonverterMap helps you keep your code clean, testable and expressive — without reflection-heavy overhead or licensing issues.

docs.title: 🔁 Recursive Mapping
docs.text:
Use the second overload of .ForMember to call .Map<T1, T2>() within the expression:

.ForMember(dest => dest.Profile, (src, map) =>
    src.Profile != null ? map.Map<Profile, ProfileDto>(src.Profile) : null
)

docs.title: 📋 Mapping Collections
docs.text:
.ForMember(dest => dest.Items, (src, map) =>
    src.Items?.Select(i => map.Map<Item, ItemDto>(i)).ToList()
)

Supports lists, arrays and other IEnumerable<>.

docs.title: 🧠 Conditional Mapping
docs.text: .When(dest => dest.Status, src => src.IsActive && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(src.Status))

docs.title: ⚠️ Null Handling
docs.text:
KonverterMap is null-safe by default. But inside custom expressions, you should still check nulls manually to avoid exceptions.

src.Address != null ? map.Map<Address, AddressDto>(src.Address) : null

docs.title: 🧩 Deep Nested Mapping
docs.text:
Supports multi-level mapping with recursive objects, like:

Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<User, UserDto>()
    .ForMember(dest => dest.Manager, (src, map) => map.Map<User, UserDto>(src.Manager));

docs.title: 📋 Changelog
docs.text: Todas as alterações feitas neste projeto estão listadas abaixo.

docs.title: v1.0.1 - 2025-04-08
docs.text:
• Added support for recursive mapping with Konverter context
  • Improved null-safety in mapping delegates
  • Fixed changelog rendering on GitHub releases

docs.title: v1.0.0 - 2025-04-06
docs.text:
• Initial release of KonverterMap
  • Added support for CreateMap, ForMember, Ignore, When, ReverseMap

docs.title: 🛠 CreateMap
docs.text:
Registers a mapping between two types.

Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<Source, Destination>();

If both types have properties with the same name and type, the mapping is automatic.

docs.title: 🧠 ForMember
docs.text: Define a custom mapping for a specific property.

docs.title: 💡 Example 1 – basic logic
docs.text: .ForMember(dest => dest.FullName, src => $"{src.FirstName} {src.LastName}")

docs.title: 💡 Example 2 – recursive mapping
docs.text: .ForMember(dest => dest.Address, (src, map) => map.Map<Address, AddressDto>(src.Address))

docs.title: 💡 Example 3 – mapping collections
docs.text:
.ForMember(dest => dest.Orders, (src, map) =>
    src.Orders?.Select(o => map.Map<Order, OrderDto>(o)).ToList()
)

docs.title: 🙈 Ignore
docs.text:
Skips a property from being mapped.

.Ignore(dest => dest.InternalCode)

docs.title: 🧪 When
docs.text:
Maps a property only if a condition is true.

.When(dest => dest.Status, src => src.IsActive)

docs.title: 🔁 ReverseMap
docs.text:
Registers the reverse mapping automatically.

Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<User, UserDto>().ReverseMap();

This is equivalent to:

Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<User, UserDto>();
Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<UserDto, User>();

docs.title: 📦 Installation
docs.text:
Install via NuGet:

dotnet add package KonverterMap

Or manually edit your .csproj:

<PackageReference Include="KonverterMap" Version="1.0.1" />

docs.title: 🚀 First Map
docs.text:
Suppose you have two classes:

public class User
{
    public string FirstName { get; set; }
    public string LastName { get; set; }
}

public class UserDto
{
    public string FullName { get; set; }
}

docs.title: 🔧 Setup the map
docs.text:
Konverter.Instance.CreateMap<User, UserDto>()
    .ForMember(dest => dest.FullName, src => $"{src.FirstName} {src.LastName}");

docs.title: ▶️ Execute mapping
docs.text:
var user = new User { FirstName = "John", LastName = "Doe" };
var dto = Konverter.Instance.Map<User, UserDto>(user);
Console.WriteLine(dto.FullName); // John Doe
